# Techdox Docs

> Practical self-hosting guides, Docker Compose tutorials, and homelab resources for the Techdox community. Companion documentation to the Techdox YouTube channel.

## Guides

- [Setting Up 2FAuth with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/2fa/): 2FAuth is a self-hosted application that enhances your security by providing a two-factor authentication system. It allows you to generate and manage 2FA codes, giving you an added layer of protection for your online accounts.
- [About Techdox Docs](https://docs.techdox.nz/about/): Welcome to Techdox Docs, the official documentation source for the Techdox YouTube channel.
- [AdGuard - Docker Setup](https://docs.techdox.nz/adguard/): AdGuard is a comprehensive, customizable network software that blocks ads, trackers, and malicious websites. It enhances user privacy and improves browsing speed and security.
- [Setting Up Authentik with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/authentik/): Authentik is an open-source Identity Provider (IdP) designed to be flexible and versatile. It offers a robust solution for authentication, authorization, and federation, enabling secure access management across your applications.
- [Deploying Beszel with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/beszel/): Beszel is a lightweight, self-hosted server monitoring hub with agent-based metrics collection. This guide details deploying Beszel using Docker Compose.
- [Deploying cAdvisor with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/cadvisor/): cAdvisor (Container Advisor) is a tool that provides insights into the resource usage and performance characteristics of running containers. This guide details deploying cAdvisor alongside Prometheus and Redis using Docker Compose, including setup instructions for monitoring container metrics.
- [Setting Up Chibisafe with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/chibisafe/): Chibisafe is a file uploader and manager that's easy to use, allowing for the quick and secure sharing of files. It's a self-hosted solution for those who need control over their file-sharing environment.
- [Deploying Cosmos Cloud Server with Docker](https://docs.techdox.nz/cosmos/): Learn how to deploy Cosmos Cloud Server with Docker — an intuitive platform for managing containers, services, and self-hosted apps.
- [Docker Containers](https://docs.techdox.nz/docker-containers/): Welcome to the Docker Containers section of Techdox Docs. Here, you'll find comprehensive documentation on each container we use and demonstrate on the Techdox YouTube channel.
- [Setting Up Dockge with Docker](https://docs.techdox.nz/dockge/): Dockge is a self-hosted, reactive Docker stack manager, offering an easy-to-use interface for managing Docker containers and compose stacks. It's created by the same developer as Uptime-Kuma.
- [Deploying Dockpeek with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/dockpeek/): Dockpeek is a self-hosted web UI for monitoring multiple Docker hosts in one place. This guide shows how to deploy Dockpeek using Docker Compose with optional remote host support.
- [Setting Up Docmost with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/docmost/): Docmost is a self-hosted document collaboration and management platform. This guide will help you set up Docmost using Docker Compose for a seamless and secure deployment.
- [Duplicati - Docker Setup](https://docs.techdox.nz/duplicati/): Duplicati is a free and open-source backup software that allows you to securely store backups online in various standard protocols and services.
- [Setting Up Excalidraw with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/excalidraw/): Excalidraw is a virtual collaborative whiteboard tool that lets you easily sketch diagrams with a hand-drawn feel. It's designed to be simple, intuitive, and to allow rapid collaboration.
- [Setting Up Filebrowser with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/filebrowser/): Filebrowser is a self-hosted file managing interface that allows you to manage files and directories through a web interface. It's a simple and convenient way to access, upload, and manage your files remotely.
- [Deploying Firefly III with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/firefly/): Firefly III is a free and open-source personal finance manager. This guide provides steps for deploying Firefly III using Docker Compose, including important notes on configuring environment variables, setting up cron jobs, and handling optional configurations.
- [Setting Up Focalboard with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/focalboard/): Focalboard is an open-source, self-hosted project management tool that's an alternative to Trello, Notion, and Asana. It's designed to help teams stay organized and aligned.
- [Setting Up FreshRSS with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/freshrss/): FreshRSS is a self-hosted RSS feed aggregator. It is lightweight, easy to work with, and allows you to keep all your favorite news feeds and blogs organized in one place.
- [General Guides](https://docs.techdox.nz/general-guides/): Welcome to the General Guides section of our self-hosting documentation.
- [Deploying Glance with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/glance/): Glance is a customizable dashboard application that aggregates content from various sources. This guide provides steps for deploying Glance using Docker Compose, including setting up the `glance.yml` configuration file and configuring environment settings.
- [Setting Up GlusterFS with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/glusterfs/): Set up GlusterFS on Ubuntu 22.04 to create a replicated, distributed filesystem shared across multiple nodes.
- [Setting Up Grafana with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/grafana/): Grafana is an open-source platform for monitoring and observability. It allows you to query, visualize, alert on, and understand your metrics no matter where they are stored.
- [Setting Up Headscale with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/headscale/): Step-by-step guide to deploying Headscale, an open-source self-hosted Tailscale control server, using Docker Compose.
- [Setting Up Homepage with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/homepage/): Homepage is a customizable start page or dashboard for your browser, designed to provide quick access to your frequently used websites, services, and tools.
- [Techdox Docs | Self-Hosting and Docker Tutorials](https://docs.techdox.nz/): Practical self-hosting guides, Docker Compose tutorials, and homelab resources for the Techdox community.
- [Setting Up IT Tools with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/ittools/): IT Tools Docker container using Docker Compose. IT Tools is a utility container providing various information technology tools.
- [Setting Up Jellyfin with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/jellyfin/): Jellyfin is a free and open-source media solution that allows you to organize, manage, and share your media files.
- [Setting Up Joplin with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/joplin/): Joplin Server is a self-hosted sync server for Joplin notes, allowing you to sync across multiple devices securely and privately.
- [Setting Up Karakeep with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/karakeep/): Karakeep (formerly Hoarder) is a self-hosted application for saving and organising bookmarks, links, and notes with AI-powered tagging. This guide explains how to deploy Karakeep using Docker Compose.
- [Setting Up Linkstack with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/linkstack/): Linkstack is a web application that provides a user-friendly platform for managing and organizing web links. It is designed for ease of use and convenience in storing a collection of links.
- [Logging in Self-Hosting](https://docs.techdox.nz/logging-overview/): Welcome to the Logging section of our self-hosting documentation.
- [Setting Up Logitech Media Server with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/logitech/): Logitech Media Server (LMS) is a software that powers audio streaming to Logitech Squeezebox players.
- [Deploying Loki and Promtail with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/loki/): Loki is a log aggregation system and Promtail is an agent that ships the logs to Loki. This guide details deploying Loki and Promtail using Docker Compose, including the steps to download necessary configuration files and set up additional hosts.
- [Self-Hosting Matrix with Docker, Element, and Voice Calls](https://docs.techdox.nz/matrix/): Deploy a private Matrix homeserver with Synapse, Element Web, Element Call, LiveKit, coturn, Nginx Proxy Manager, and Cloudflare Tunnel. Full compose file, all config files, and voice/video setup included.
- [Deploying Mealie with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/mealie/): Mealie is a self-hosted recipe manager and meal planner. This guide covers deploying Mealie with Docker Compose including persistent storage and resource limits.
- [Setting Up Memos with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/memos/): Memos is a self-hosted note-taking application that offers a convenient way to organize and store personal notes, memos, and other pieces of information.
- [Setting Up MkDocs with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/mkdocs/): MkDocs is a static site generator that's geared towards project documentation. With the Material theme, it provides a sleek, responsive, and user-friendly interface for your documentation projects.
- [Networking in Self-Hosting](https://docs.techdox.nz/networking-overview/): Welcome to the Networking section of our self-hosting documentation.
- [Setting Up Nextcloud with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/nextcloud/): Nextcloud is an open-source, self-hosted file share and collaboration platform. It provides a secure and private alternative to cloud-based storage services.
- [Deploying Nexterm with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/nexterm/): Nexterm is an open-source server management software that allows you to manage SSH, VNC, and RDP connections. This guide provides steps for deploying Nexterm using Docker Compose.
- [Setting Up Node Exporter](https://docs.techdox.nz/node-exporter/): The Node Exporter is a project that is maintained through the Prometheus project.
- [Setting Up Nginx Proxy Manager with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/npm/): Nginx Proxy Manager (NPM) simplifies the process of managing Nginx proxy configurations for web services.
- [Deploying ntfy with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/ntfy/): ntfy is a simple and flexible notification service that allows you to send notifications to devices via web, email, or apps. This guide details deploying ntfy using Docker Compose, including configuration settings and health checks.
- [Setting Up Pairdrop with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/pairdrop/): Pairdrop is an application designed for simple and secure file sharing. It's a self-hosted solution that allows easy file transfers within a network.
- [Self-Hosting Pangolin with Installer](https://docs.techdox.nz/pangolin/): Learn how to deploy Pangolin, a secure and self-hosted alternative to Cloudflare Tunnels, using the official installer.
- [Setting Up Paperless-ngx with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/paperless/): Paperless-ngx is an open-source document management tool designed to digitize your physical documents into a searchable archive. It supports a wide range of hardware architectures including amd64, arm, and arm64.
- [Setting Up Peppermint with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/peppermint/): Peppermint is a self-hosted ticketing and knowledge base solution. This guide explains how to deploy Peppermint using Docker Compose.
- [Setting Up PhotoPrism with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/photoprism/): Learn how to deploy PhotoPrism using Docker Compose to easily manage your photo collections with powerful AI capabilities.
- [Deploying PiHole Exporter with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/piexporter/): This guide provides step-by-step instructions for deploying PiHole Exporter using Docker Compose, including configuring the environment variables and integrating with Prometheus and Grafana for visualization.
- [Access Pi-hole via Nginx Proxy Manager](https://docs.techdox.nz/pihole-on-npm/): How to configure Nginx Proxy Manager to proxy Pi-hole, including domain setup, SSL, and custom location configuration.
- [Deploying Pingvin Share with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/pingvin/): Set up Pingvin Share, a lightweight self-hosted file sharing service, using Docker Compose.
- [Deploying Pocket ID with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/pocketid/): Pocket ID is a self-hosted, privacy-focused identity management solution. This guide provides step-by-step instructions for deploying Pocket ID using Docker Compose, including the required Docker Compose configuration and environment variables.
- [Setting Up PrivateBin with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/privatebin/): PrivateBin is a secure, minimalist, open-source online pastebin where the server has zero knowledge of pasted data. It allows you to share sensitive information securely and anonymously.
- [Setting Up Prometheus with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/prometheus/): Prometheus is an open-source monitoring and alerting toolkit, widely used for its powerful querying language, efficient storage, and ease of integration with various metrics sources.
- [RackNerd Hosting Deals](https://docs.techdox.nz/racknerd/): Current RackNerd VPS, shared, and reseller hosting deals with affiliate links. Great value hosting for self-hosted projects and homelabs.
- [Deploying Safeline with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/safeline/): Safeline is a web application firewall that provides advanced protection against web threats. This guide provides steps for deploying Safeline using Docker Compose, setting up the required `.env` file, and breaking down the provided Compose configuration.
- [Deploying Speedtest Tracker with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/speedtest-tracker/): Speedtest Tracker is a self-hosted internet performance tracking application that runs speedtest checks against Ookla's Speedtest service.
- [Start Here - Self-Hosting from Zero](https://docs.techdox.nz/start-here/): New to self-hosting? This guide takes you from nothing to your first running service - hardware, Ubuntu, Docker, and your first container, step by step.
- [Setting Up Stirling PDF with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/stirling-pdf/): Stirling PDF is a self-hosted PDF toolbox for merging, splitting, compressing, converting, OCRing, signing, and editing PDFs from your own server.
- [Support Techdox Docs](https://docs.techdox.nz/support/): Support Techdox Docs through donations or buying a coffee to help maintain and expand our self-hosting and Docker guides.
- [Setting Up Syncthing with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/syncthing/): Syncthing is an open-source continuous file synchronization program. It synchronizes files between two or more computers in real time, safely and efficiently.
- [Browse by Tag](https://docs.techdox.nz/tags/): Browse every Techdox guide by topic - Docker, monitoring, networking, security, media, productivity, and more.
- [Secure Web Services with Traefik and Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/traefik/): Traefik is a reverse proxy which allows for seamless service communication
- [Deploying Tududi with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/tududi/): Tududi is a lightweight, open-source task management tool. This guide provides step-by-step instructions for deploying Tududi using Docker Compose, including setting up the required directory, creating a `docker-compose.yml` file, and configuring the environment variables for secure and seamless operation.
- [Setting Up Uptime Kuma with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/uptimekuma/): Uptime Kuma is a self-hosted monitoring tool similar to "Uptime Robot." It monitors your websites, services, and applications and provides uptime notifications.
- [Deploying Wallos with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/wallos/): Wallos is a powerful open-source subscription tracker and financial management tool. This guide provides steps for deploying Wallos using Docker Compose, with important notes on setting up volumes to avoid permissions errors.
- [Setting Up Watchtower with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/watchtower/): Watchtower is an application that automatically updates your running Docker containers to the latest images. It monitors all containers and updates them in real-time whenever a new image is released.
- [Deploying WG-Easy with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/wgeasy/): WG-Easy is a simple, web-based management interface for WireGuard VPN.
- [Setting Up Wireguard and Wireguard UI with Docker Compose](https://docs.techdox.nz/wireguard/): Wireguard is a modern VPN (Virtual Private Network) software that provides fast and secure connections. The Wireguard UI is a web interface that makes it easier to manage your Wireguard setup.

## Homelab Rebuild Series

- [Full Stack Architecture Diagram](https://docs.techdox.nz/homelab-rebuild/architecture/): Complete architecture diagram of the homelab rebuild stack, showing all layers from network to identity.
- [Cloudflare Tunnels for Public Services](https://docs.techdox.nz/homelab-rebuild/cloudflare-tunnels/): Expose self-hosted services to the internet without opening a single port. Cloudflare Tunnels create an outbound-only connection from cloudflared to Cloudflare's edge.
- [Pi-hole + Unbound DNS Stack](https://docs.techdox.nz/homelab-rebuild/dns-stack/): Build a fully self-contained DNS stack with Pi-hole for ad blocking and Unbound for recursive resolution. No queries leave to Cloudflare, Google, or your ISP.
- [Homelab Rebuild 2026 — Overview & Architecture](https://docs.techdox.nz/homelab-rebuild/): A complete rebuild of a personal homelab and identity stack, covering open-source routing, full DNS sovereignty, encrypted remote access, zero exposed ports, and identity privacy.
- [Internal Hostnames with NPM and Pi-hole](https://docs.techdox.nz/homelab-rebuild/internal-hostnames/): Serve internal self-hosted services at real domain names with HTTPS using Nginx Proxy Manager and Pi-hole local DNS records. No public exposure required.
- [macOS Security Hardening](https://docs.techdox.nz/homelab-rebuild/macos-hardening/): Practical macOS hardening using native protections and the Objective-See free toolkit. No third-party antivirus required.
- [Migrating to OPNsense on Zimaboard 2](https://docs.techdox.nz/homelab-rebuild/opnsense-zimaboard/): How to replace a consumer mesh router with OPNsense running on a Zimaboard 2, covering hardware, install, DHCP with Dnsmasq, host overrides, and the physical cutover.
- [Migrating Email to Proton Mail with Custom Domain](https://docs.techdox.nz/homelab-rebuild/proton-mail-custom-domain/): Step-by-step guide to migrating your email from any provider to Proton Mail with a custom domain, including MX, SPF, DKIM, DMARC setup and importing historical mail.
- [WireGuard VPN on OPNsense](https://docs.techdox.nz/homelab-rebuild/wireguard-vpn/): Set up WireGuard directly on your OPNsense router for kernel-level VPN performance, native firewall integration, and secure remote access with split or full tunnel support.
- [YubiKey FIDO2 SSH Authentication](https://docs.techdox.nz/homelab-rebuild/yubikey-ssh/): Set up hardware-bound SSH authentication using a YubiKey and FIDO2 ed25519-sk keys. Works on macOS with Homebrew OpenSSH, requires physical key presence for every login.
